channel.title: Primary Source on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Primary Source on RaihnForge

item.title: Destruction of the Indies — las Casas and the Gap Between Profession and Practice
item.description: Bartolomé de las Casas’s Destruction of the Indies is an appeal letter to the Spanish monarch, written by a friar who has seen what is being done in the king’s name and decided he can no longer be quiet about it. As a primary source it is striking because the contrast is doing all the work: the people described as gentle, and the people sent to save them described as predators.

item.title: Code of Hammurabi — Reading a Mesopotamian Society Through Its Laws
item.description:
The Code of Hammurabi reads less like a list of rules and more like a cross-section of a society. The punishments are not interchangeable. Who you injured, and what class they belonged to, decided what you owed. Reading the code as a primary source means letting the price list do the talking.

Social Hierarchy: Reading the Price List

There seems to be a patriarchal class social hierarchy based on the Code of Hammurabi. Men, women, plebeian status, or slave status are often noted in the punishments of violations. For instance:
